title: Personal taxes
description: Practice understanding of personal taxes, with focus on understanding of taxable vs non taxable, interest, dividends, and capital gains.

assessment_items.question_content:
Apart from paying taxes on the income from your job, you must also pay taxes on interest.

**Where does taxable interest come from?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Apart from paying taxes on the income from your job, you must also pay taxes on interest.

**Which tax rate applies to interest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Any money you make, whether it is from savings account, or sale of property, is taxed.

**Which tax do you pay on any interest you earn from savings accounts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Apart from paying taxes on the income from your job, you must also taxes on any dividends you receive.

**Where do dividends come from?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Apart from paying taxes on the income from your job, you must also pay taxes on dividends.

**Which tax rate applies to dividends?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Any money you make, whether it is from savings account, or dividends, is taxed.

**Which tax do you pay on dividends?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Although "capital gains" might sound like something only companies deal with, regular folks pay capital gain taxes, too.

**On which of these would you pay capital gains taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A company you work for gave all their employees shares of company stock. Years later, you may decide to sell these stocks.

**If you sell these stocks for a profit, what tax will you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are two different tax rates for profits from selling investments.

**What determines which tax rate you pay when you decide to sell an investment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how long you owned the investment

assessment_items.question_content:
When you earn money or make purchases, the taxes you pay can differ quite a bit. 

**What is the tax range for any interest you earn?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you earn money or make purchases, the taxes you pay can differ quite a bit. 

**What is the tax range for dividends you earn?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you earn money or make purchases, the taxes you pay can differ quite a bit. 

**If you sell investments after holding them for a long time, what tax rate will you pay on those profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
